execution_summary: We built the custom Python 3.10-slim image and launched the container successfully. The analysis entry-point (replication_data/analysis_petersen2017__py.py) failed repeatedly because the pandas Excel reader could not locate an importable copy of openpyxl. We modified replication_info.json to add the dependency and regenerated / rebuilt the Docker image (v3). Despite pip installing openpyxl, pandas still raised “Missing optional dependency openpyxl”. We then patched the analysis script to ensure openpyxl could be loaded (and, if absent, installed at run-time). This second patch unfortunately introduced duplicate import statements that produced a SyntaxError. Owing to the cascading code-edit conflicts the analysis never executed, therefore no statistical results were produced during this debugging session.

code_executed.logs: Dockerfile generated with base image python:3.10-slim and pip installs (numpy, pandas, scipy, openpyxl).
Image built completely with openpyxl pre-installed.
code_executed.environment: Docker (Python 3.10-slim, numpy 1.23.5, pandas 1.5.3, scipy 1.9.3, openpyxl installed by pip)

code_executed.logs: ImportError: Missing optional dependency 'openpyxl' raised by pandas.read_excel even though openpyxl was installed; script aborted after printing 'Found 48 participants'.
SyntaxError caused by duplicated / malformed import lines introduced during hot-patching of the script; execution halted before any analysis.

results.hypothesis_tested: Presenting an 85 dB auditory cue immediately before the visual target increases TVA visual processing speed (v) and reduces perceptual threshold (t0) compared to a no-cue baseline.
